summary: ollama-helpers postinstall harvests developer identity and cloud config, exfiltrates to attacker endpoint
details: scripts/postinstall.js runs automatically on npm install and collects a broad set of installer-side identity and cloud-context data: os.hostname(), os.userInfo() username, git user.email from ~/.gitconfig, GitHub CLI identity from ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml, SSH public-key comments from ~/.ssh/*.pub, git remote origin and recent reflog committer emails, GCP project and authenticated account from ~/.config/gcloud/properties, AWS profile names from ~/.aws/config, DNS search domain from /etc/resolv.conf, current working directory, and parent project package.json metadata. The harvested bundle is HTTPS-POSTed to npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app, a Google Cloud Run endpoint unrelated to the package's claimed publisher. Inline comments falsely state that only platform/Node/architecture are collected and that no emails, tokens, or credentials are transmitted — the actual payload includes multiple email addresses, cloud account identifiers, and SSH key comments. The package name and metadata (author 'Ollama JS Dev', homepage ollama-js.dev, repo ollama-js-dev/ollama-helpers) impersonate the Ollama ecosystem and are not affiliated with the official Ollama project. The 'telemetry' framing with an opt-out env var is consent-washing over targeted reconnaissance of developer cloud and SCM identity.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
